canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, Wikipedia is employed as a measurement instrument with multiple applications. It is explicitly defined as an "external knowledge base" from which documents are retrieved for RAG evaluation, with one study specifying the use of a "Wikimedia dump provided by Cohere". In addition to its role as a knowledge source for retrieval, other papers use Wikipedia to measure distinct tasks. The provided data shows it is used as an instrument to evaluate both "Language modeling" and "Node classification".
papers: Ambiguity Awareness Optimization: Towards Semantic Disambiguation for Direct Preference Optimization
CAT: Causal Attention Tuning For Injecting Fine-grained Causal Knowledge into Large Language Models
Faster In-Context Learning forLLMs via N-Gram Trie Speculative Decoding

definitions.text: Wikipedia, used as the external knowledge base from which retrieved documents are drawn for RAG evaluation.
